channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOGE has disregarded data protection and privacy norms. The consequences will be felt years down the line
item.description: It has been a tumultuous few weeks since Donald Trump took office for the second time as president of the United States, While Trump has garnered headlines for his outlandish executive orders aimed at recasting the country in his own shape, Elon Musk, the tech billionaire turned government quango-bu
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: How to strengthen the security of Oracle applications without relying on OAU
item.description: Vendor support agreements have long been a sticking point for customers, and the Oracle Applications Unlimited (OAU) program is no different.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google’s online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lauren Sanchez alludes to ‘turbulence’ in life after ‘obscene’ Trump inauguration display
item.description: The fiancee of billionaire Jeff Bezos alluded to a rough start of 2025, several weeks after Megyn Kelly called her a 'hooker' for wearing a lacy white bustier top beneath a jacket to Trump's swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Paris AI Action Summit: US and UK refuse to sign accord
item.description: The escalating electricity demands of artificial intelligence systems are raising concerns about the technology’s sustainability — but that’s apparently of little concern to the governments of the US and the UK.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI agent could help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help customers with one of the more difficult and confusing purchase decisions: buying a car.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The European Commission Unveils €200 Billion Plan for AI Development | InvestAI
item.description: The European Commission has announced the mobilization of €200 billion for the InvestAI plan, aiming to enable open and collaborative development of artificial intelligence in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Keen to buy a new gaming laptop with an RTX 5000 GPU? Save the date: Nvidia’s announced pre-orders open on February 25
item.description: Can’t buy an RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU? At least you’ll soon be able to pre-order a gaming laptop with one inside.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists say several thousand earthquakes detected near Greece's island of Santorini
item.description: Scientists have detected several thousand earthquakes, the vast majority of them with small magnitudes, in just over two weeks near Greece’s volcanic island of Santorini.
item.dc:creator: Elena Becatoros, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: All your messages in one place: the new Besties app for Google Pixel will bring together chats from Google Messages, WhatsApp, and other apps
item.description: Google is adding its new Besties app to Pixel phones, allowing users to centralize messages from their closest friends across various messaging services
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Opinion: Tesla’s awful numbers put Musk back into campaign mode
item.description: Very little looks good on EV company's balance sheet, but Tesla chairman tries every way possible to spark excitement.
item.dc:creator: Liam Denning, The Mercury News

item.title: How Elon Musk’s crusade against government could benefit Tesla
item.description: The Trump administration has not yet declared any actions that could benefit Tesla or Musk’s other companies. However, snuffing out federal investigations or jettisoning safety initiatives would be an easier task than their assault on regulators and the bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 1.5 will add Human Torch and The Thing later this month as NetEase walks back its plans for a mid-season rank reset
item.description: Marvel Rivals Season 1.5 will be released on February 21, 2025 and will add Human Torch and The Thing.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fresh M4 MacBook Air rumors point to an imminent launch, but it could be a modest update
item.description: Apple’s M4 MacBook Air has had its chip identifier number revealed, suggesting its launch is imminent.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: NASA astronauts are streaming live on Twitch from space. Here’s how to watch
item.description: Ever wondered what life is like for an astronaut? Now you can ask during NASA’s first Twitch event, where astronauts will be broadcasting live from the International Space Station (ISS). The stream is set to take place on Wednesday, February 12, at 11:45 a.m. ET on NASA’s official Twitch channel.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI summit in Paris brings together Big Tech and U.S. VP Vance
item.description: Major world leaders are meeting for an AI summit in Paris, where challenging diplomatic talks are expected as tech titans fight for dominance in the fast-moving technology industry.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Roblox joins $27 million industry nonprofit to support online safety
item.description: A group of internet businesses, including Roblox, Google, OpenAI, and Discord, have cofounded a nonprofit called Robust Open Online Safety Tools (ROOST).
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: Intel invested $16.7 billion when it bought FPGA maker Altera in 2016, while AMD spent upwards of $35 billion in 2020 to acquire Xilinx, but the vendors have little if anything to show for it.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming more like Facebook
item.description: Microsoft Teams users can expect a slew of new features in an upcoming update that is currently in full testing, including Storyline, a Facebook-like feed where users can share messages, news, or congratulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech unemployment jumps, even as job openings increase
item.description: The unemployment rate among tech workers leaped almost a full percentage point from December to January, according to an analysis of US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Notre Dame scores big with Wi-Fi 6E stadium upgrade
item.description: When 80,000 fans fill the University of Notre Dame’s iconic football stadium on Saturday afternoons in the fall to cheer on the Fighting Irish, there’s no shortage of high-decibel screaming. And now, thanks to a major network expansion, there’s no shortage of high-bandwidth streaming.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk calls for annual firing of judges after blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called for the annual firing of judges following an early Saturday decision from a judge stating that the Treasury Department should bar access to its payment systems to anyone besides “civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beleaguered Cal State University’s $17 million artificial intelligence initiative defended, attacked
item.description: The California State University system, facing an estimated budget shortfall of $428 million to $1 billion next year because of state-level cuts and deferments, this week announced a partnership with major technology companies including OpenAI, Google and Microsoft to bring their artificial intellig
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KRON4
